questions.text: What is the significance of the 'Muqatta'at' (disconnected letters) like 'Alif, Lam, Mim' at the start of the Surah?
questions.answer: They are a challenge to the master linguists of Arabia, showing that while the Quran uses their letters, they cannot replicate its divine origin. Their specific meanings are known only to Allah.

questions.text: In the opening, for whom is the Quran described as a 'guidance'?
questions.answer: It is a guidance for the 'Muttaqin' (those conscious of Allah/the God-fearing).

questions.text: True or False: Belief in the 'Ghaib' (Unseen) is a fundamental characteristic of the Muttaqin.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: The Surah describes three types of people in the first 20 verses. Name them.
questions.answer: The Believers (Mu'minun), the Disbelievers (Kafirun), and the Hypocrites (Munafiqun).

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Baqarah describe the 'sickness' in the hearts of the hypocrites?
questions.answer: It is a sickness of doubt and hypocrisy which Allah increases because of their persistence in lying and deception.

questions.text: What parable is used to describe the hypocrites' state of confusion regarding the light of guidance?
questions.answer: The parable of someone who kindles a fire but then loses its light, and the parable of a rainstorm with darkness, thunder, and lightning.

questions.text: What is the very first command (Amr) addressed to all of mankind in this Surah?
questions.answer: The command to 'Worship your Lord, who created you and those before you' ().

questions.text: What challenge does Allah pose to those who doubt the authenticity of the Quran?
questions.answer: To produce even a single Surah similar to it and to call upon their witnesses besides Allah if they are truthful.

questions.text: Why did the Angels question Allah when He announced the creation of a 'Khalifah' (vicegerent) on Earth?
questions.answer: They feared the new creation would spread corruption and shed blood, based on their observation of previous inhabitants or general nature.

questions.text: What specific ability did Allah give Adam (AS) to prove his superiority over the Angels?
questions.answer: Allah taught Adam the names of all things (knowledge/intellect).

questions.text: Who refused to prostrate to Adam and what was the reason given for the refusal?
questions.answer: Iblis (Satan) refused out of arrogance (Kibr), believing he was superior because he was made of fire and Adam of clay.

questions.text: What was the immediate consequence for Adam and Hawa (Eve) after eating from the forbidden tree?
questions.answer: Their private parts became apparent to them, and they were sent down from the Garden to dwell on Earth.

questions.text: Did Adam (AS) remain in a state of sin after the fall, or was he forgiven?
questions.answer: He was forgiven after receiving specific words of repentance from Allah and turning back to Him.

questions.text: Why does the Surah address the Children of Israel (Bani Israel) extensively?
questions.answer: To provide lessons from their history, warn the Muslims against repeating their mistakes, and remind them of the fulfilled prophecies regarding Prophet Muhammad.

questions.text: What was the 'Covenant' Allah took from the Children of Israel that they violated?
questions.answer: To worship only Allah, be good to parents and kin, establish prayer, and give Zakat.

questions.text: Explain the significance of the incident of the 'Cow' (Al-Baqarah) from which the Surah takes its name.
questions.answer: It involved a murder mystery where Allah commanded the sacrifice of a cow to identify the killer, testing their obedience as they tried to avoid the command with excessive questioning.

questions.text: What does the phrase 'their hearts became like stones or even harder' refer to?
questions.answer: It refers to the Children of Israel whose hearts became hardened against signs, unlike stones which sometimes gush water or fall out of fear of Allah.

questions.text: What did the people of Prophet Musa (AS) demand to see before they would believe?
questions.answer: They demanded to see Allah 'plainly' (manifestly), resulting in them being struck by a thunderbolt.

questions.text: Which two angels were sent to Babylon as a trial for the people regarding magic (Sihr)?
questions.answer: Harut and Marut.

questions.text: What is the Quranic stance on abrogation (naskh) in revelation?
questions.answer: Allah teaches that when a ruling or recitation is replaced, He brings what is better or similar—showing wisdom and mercy in legislation.

questions.text: Which Prophet is credited with raising the foundations of the Kaaba with his son?
questions.answer: Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) and his son Isma'il (Ishmael).

questions.text: What was the prayer of Ibrahim (AS) for the city of Makkah and its future people?
questions.answer: He prayed for its security, for its people to have provision, and for a Messenger to be raised from among them.

questions.text: What is the 'Millah' (religion/way) of Ibrahim described as in this Surah?
questions.answer: The way of 'Hanif' (pure monotheism), being one who submits entirely to Allah.

questions.text: Where was the original Qiblah (direction of prayer) before it was changed to the Kaaba?
questions.answer: Bait al-Maqdis (Jerusalem).

questions.text: What does Allah say is the reason for changing the Qiblah?
questions.answer: To test the people and distinguish those who follow the Messenger from those who turn on their heels.

questions.text: Does Allah expect the believers to be tested? If so, with what?
questions.answer: Yes, with fear, hunger, loss of wealth, lives, and fruits.

questions.text: What is the 'Safa and Marwa' referred to in the Surah?
questions.answer: They are among the symbols (Sha'a'ir) of Allah, and it is no sin to perform the walk between them during Hajj or Umrah.

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who hide the clear signs and guidance Allah has revealed?
questions.answer: They are cursed by Allah and cursed by the angels and the believers.

questions.text: What are the four things mentioned that are forbidden (Haram) to eat?
questions.answer: Dead animals (carrion), blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah.

questions.text: How does the Quran define 'Al-Birr' (True Righteousness)?
questions.answer: Believing in Allah, the Last Day, Angels, Books, Prophets, giving wealth despite loving it, establishing prayer, giving Zakat, fulfilling contracts, and being patient.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of Fasting (Siyam) as stated in the Surah?
questions.answer: So that you may attain 'Taqwa' (God-consciousness).

questions.text: In which month was the Quran revealed?
questions.answer: The month of Ramadan.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His proximity to the believer in the context of Dua (supplication)?
questions.answer: He says: 'Indeed I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me'.

questions.text: What is the ruling on fighting during the Sacred Months?
questions.answer: Fighting is a great sin therein, but preventing people from the way of Allah and expelling them from the Masjid al-Haram is even greater.

questions.text: What is the 'best provision' for the journey of Hajj?
questions.answer: Taqwa (God-consciousness/piety).

questions.text: What are the two types of people mentioned regarding their supplications?
questions.answer: Those who only ask for the good of this world, and those who ask for 'good in this world, good in the Hereafter, and protection from the Fire'.

questions.text: Who is the person described as 'the most quarrelsome of opponents' (Aladdul Khisam)?
questions.answer: The hypocrite who speaks well of this life and calls Allah to witness what is in his heart, but spreads corruption on earth once he leaves.

questions.text: Does the Surah allow a believer to enter Islam partially?
questions.answer: No, it commands: 'Enter into Islam completely (Kaffah)' and do not follow the footsteps of Satan.

questions.text: What is the 'root' reason given for why people began to differ in religion after being one nation?
questions.answer: Envy and animosity (Baghyan) between themselves after knowledge had come to them.

questions.text: Is it permissible to marry a polytheist (Mushrik)?
questions.answer: No, until they believe; a believing slave is better than a free polytheist even if the latter attracts you.

questions.text: What is the 'Iddah' (waiting period) for a divorced woman who has menstrual cycles?
questions.answer: Three menstrual periods (Quru').

questions.text: How many times can a divorce (Talaq) be revocable before it becomes final?
questions.answer: Two times. After the third, it is final.

questions.text: What is the recommended duration for breastfeeding a child?
questions.answer: Two whole years for those who wish to complete the term.

questions.text: What is the waiting period for a widow (whose husband has died)?
questions.answer: Four months and ten days.

questions.text: Which prayer is specifically emphasized to be guarded?
questions.answer: The 'middle prayer' (Al-Salat al-Wusta), generally understood as the 'Asr prayer.

questions.text: In the story of Talut (Saul) and Jalut (Goliath), how did Allah test the army of Talut?
questions.answer: By a river; they were told not to drink from it except a handful, but most of them drank abundantly.

questions.text: Who killed Jalut (Goliath)?
questions.answer: Prophet Dawud (David) (AS).

questions.text: What is the name of the greatest ayah in the Quran found in this Surah?
questions.answer: Ayat al-Kursi (the Throne Ayah).

questions.text: List three attributes of Allah mentioned in Ayat al-Kursi.
questions.answer: Al-Hayy (The Ever-Living), Al-Qayyum (The Sustainer of all), and that neither slumber nor sleep overtakes Him.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about compulsion in religion?
questions.answer: 'There is no compulsion in religion' because the right way has become clear from the wrong.

questions.text: Describe the parable of the man who passed by a city in ruins and doubted how Allah could bring it to life.
questions.answer: Allah caused him to die for a hundred years, then revived him, showing him his food had not spoiled while his donkey had become bones, then reconstructed the donkey.

questions.text: How did Ibrahim (AS) ask Allah to show him how He gives life to the dead?
questions.answer: Allah commanded him to take four birds, train them, distribute them on hills, then call them, and they came flying to him.

questions.text: What is the parable of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah?
questions.answer: A grain of corn which grows seven ears, each ear having a hundred grains (700-fold increase).

questions.text: What two things nullify the reward of charity according to the Surah?
questions.answer: Reminders of generosity (Mann) and injury/insult (Adha).

questions.text: How does Satan (Shaytan) threaten those who want to give charity?
questions.answer: He threatens them with poverty and orders them to immorality.

questions.text: What is the Quranic stance on 'Riba' (Usury/Interest)?
questions.answer: It is strictly forbidden; those who consume it are compared to one beaten by Satan into insanity, and Allah declares war against those who do not give it up.

questions.text: What is the longest ayah in the Quran about?
questions.answer: Contracts and debts, emphasizing writing them down and having witnesses.

questions.text: Does Allah charge a soul with more than it can bear?
questions.answer: No, Allah does not charge a soul except within its capacity.

questions.text: True or False: We are allowed to make distinctions between the Messengers of Allah by believing in some and rejecting others.
questions.answer: False. The believers say, 'We make no distinction between any of His messengers'.

questions.text: What is the closing supplication of the Surah asking for regarding our mistakes?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred... and pardon us; forgive us; and have mercy upon us'.

questions.text: What does the name 'Al-Baqarah' mean in English?
questions.answer: The Cow.

questions.text: How is the Quran described in the early in the Surah regarding its authenticity?
questions.answer: As a Book 'about which there is no doubt' (La Rayba Fih).

questions.text: What is the specific punishment mentioned for the disbelievers in the early verses?
questions.answer: A 'great punishment' and that Allah has set a seal upon their hearts and hearing.

questions.text: What do the hypocrites claim they are doing while they are actually spreading corruption?
questions.answer: They claim: 'We are only reformers' (Innama nahnu muslihun).

questions.text: True or False: The hypocrites are described as 'purchasing error at the price of guidance'.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: What happens to the hypocrites' 'light' in the parable of the fire?
questions.answer: Allah takes away their light and leaves them in darkness so they could not see.

questions.text: What is the fuel of the Hellfire mentioned in the challenge against the Quran?
questions.answer: Men and stones.

questions.text: What are the 'pure spouses' promised to the believers in Paradise?
questions.answer: Spouses free from all physical and character impurities.

questions.text: How does Allah respond to those who question why He uses parables of small things like gnats/mosquitoes?
questions.answer: He says it is a test; He misleads many thereby and guides many thereby, but misleads only the defiantly disobedient.

questions.text: What is the 'Covenant of Allah' that the losers break?
questions.answer: The natural and revealed oath of obedience to Allah and maintaining ties of kinship.

questions.text: What question does Allah ask man to reflect on his disbelief in the face of life and death?
questions.answer: 'How can you disbelieve in Allah when you were lifeless and He brought you to life; then He will cause you to die, then He will bring you [back] to life?'

questions.text: Who did Allah tell the Angels about the names of things after Adam excelled?
questions.answer: He told the Angels that He knows what they do not know and proved it through Adam's knowledge.

questions.text: What was the specific command given to Adam regarding the Garden and the 'tree'?
questions.answer: To eat freely from anywhere but not to approach 'this tree' or become wrongdoers.

questions.text: How did Satan cause Adam and Hawa to slip?
questions.answer: By deceiving/whispering to them, which led to their removal from the state they were in.

questions.text: What is the 'Guidance' Allah promised to send down to Adam's descendants on earth?
questions.answer: Revelations and Messengers; those who follow them will have no fear nor will they grieve.

questions.text: What does Allah command the Children of Israel regarding the 'Truth' in their scriptures?
questions.answer: 'Do not mix the truth with falsehood or conceal the truth while you know [it]'.

questions.text: What should one seek help through, as mentioned multiple times in the Surah?
questions.answer: Patience (Sabr) and Prayer (Salat).

questions.text: Why is the command to be patient and pray described as 'difficult'?
questions.answer: It is difficult except for the 'Khashi'un' (those humbly submissive to Allah).

questions.text: Who are the 'Khashi'un'?
questions.answer: Those who are certain that they will meet their Lord and that to Him they will return.

questions.text: What was the 'miracle of the sea' for the Children of Israel?
questions.answer: Allah parted the sea for them, saved them, and drowned the people of Pharaoh while they were looking on.

questions.text: For how many nights did Musa (AS) go to meet Allah?
questions.answer: Forty nights.

questions.text: What did the people do in Musa's absence?
questions.answer: They took the calf for worship, committing a great wrong.

questions.text: What was the unique 'repentance' commanded to those who worshipped the calf?
questions.answer: They were commanded to kill those among themselves who were guilty (as a capital punishment/purification).

questions.text: What did Allah provide as shade for the Children of Israel in the desert?
questions.answer: Clouds.

questions.text: What foods were provided to them in the wilderness?
questions.answer: Manna and Salwa (quail).

questions.text: What happened when Musa (AS) struck the rock with his staff for water?
questions.answer: Twelve springs gushed forth, one for each tribe.

questions.text: Why did the Children of Israel ask for 'herbs, cucumbers, garlic, lentils, and onions'?
questions.answer: They were dissatisfied with the divine food (Manna and Salwa) and wanted the lowly foods of the earth they were used to in Egypt.

questions.text: What was the consequence of their constant 'disobedience and transgression'?
questions.answer: They were covered with humiliation and poverty and drew upon themselves the wrath of Allah.

questions.text: Does Allah reward the Sabians and Christians who truly believe and do good?
questions.answer: Yes, 'whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day and does righteousness - will have their reward with their Lord'.

questions.text: What happened at Mount Tur regarding the Covenant?
questions.answer: Allah raised the mountain over them and told them to 'take what We have given you with determination'.

questions.text: What were the 'Sabbath breakers' transformed into?
questions.answer: Apes, despised.

questions.text: Describe the physical characteristics of the 'Cow' that the Jews were eventually told to sacrifice.
questions.answer: It was to be a yellow cow, bright in color, pleasing to observers, neither old nor young, and never used for plowing or watering the fields.

questions.text: In the murder case of the Cow story, how did the dead man come back to life?
questions.answer: He was struck with a piece of the sacrificed cow by Allah's command.

questions.text: What did the 'illiterate' among the People of the Book know of the Scripture?
questions.answer: Only wishful thinking and hearsay (Amaniyya).

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who write the Scripture with their own hands and say 'This is from Allah'?
questions.answer: 'Woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for what they earn'.

questions.text: What was the Jews' claim about how long they would stay in Hellfire?
questions.answer: They claimed: 'The Fire will never touch us, except for a few numbered days'.

questions.text: True or False: Surah Al-Baqarah says that 'whoever earns evil and is surrounded by his sin' is a companion of the Fire.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: What was the command regarding speech with people in the Covenant?
questions.answer: 'Speak to people good [words]'.

questions.text: How did the Children of Israel treat the Messengers who brought what they did not desire?
questions.answer: They became arrogant; some they denied and others they killed.

questions.text: What did they say when invited to believe in the Quran?
questions.answer: 'We believe in what was revealed to us,' while they rejected what came after it, even though it was the truth.

questions.text: Who is the enemy of Angel Jibreel (Gabriel)?
questions.answer: The disbelievers among the Jews (as Jibreel brings the revelation they disliked).

questions.text: What is the ruling on being an enemy to Allah, His angels, and His messengers?
questions.answer: Allah is an enemy to the disbelievers.

questions.text: What did the people learn from magic in Babylon that caused social harm?
questions.answer: How to cause separation between a man and his wife.

questions.text: Can magic harm anyone without Allah's permission?
questions.answer: No, 'they do not harm anyone through it except by permission of Allah'.

questions.text: What word were the believers told to use instead of 'Ra'ina' (listen to us) to avoid ambiguity?
questions.answer: 'Unzurna' (look at us/observe us).

questions.text: What do the People of the Book wish to do to the believers out of envy?
questions.answer: To turn them back into disbelievers after they have believed.

questions.text: What is the 'legacy' or 'face' one must submit to Allah to have no fear?
questions.answer: 'Whoever submits his face to Allah while he is a doer of good' (Muhsin).

questions.text: What did the Jews and Christians say about each other's validity?
questions.answer: The Jews said the Christians have nothing to stand on, and the Christians said the Jews have nothing to stand on.

questions.text: Who is more unjust than the one who prevents the name of Allah from being mentioned in His mosques?
questions.answer: No one; they will have disgrace in this world and a great punishment in the next.

questions.text: To whom belong the East and the West?
questions.answer: To Allah; so wherever you turn, there is the Face of Allah.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His 'will' when He decides a matter?
questions.answer: He only says to it, 'Be,' and it is.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim (AS) tested with before being made an 'Imam' (leader) for mankind?
questions.answer: With certain words/commands (Kalimat) which he fulfilled.

questions.text: What did Ibrahim and Isma'il pray for as they built the Kaaba?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, accept [this] from us. Indeed, You are the Hearing, the Knowing'.

questions.text: What did Ya'qub (Jacob) ask his sons at the time of his death?
questions.answer: 'What will you worship after me?'

questions.text: What was the answer of Ya'qub's sons to his question?
questions.answer: 'We will worship your God and the God of your fathers... the One God. And we are Muslims [submitting] to Him'.

questions.text: What is 'Sibghatullah'?
questions.answer: The 'color' or 'religion' of Allah; and who is better than Allah in coloring/ordaining religion?

questions.text: What is the 'Middle Nation' (Ummatan Wasatan)?
questions.answer: The Muslim Ummah, described as balanced and just, to be witnesses over mankind.

questions.text: Did the Prophet (SAW) know people would turn back when the Qiblah was changed?
questions.answer: Allah informed him it would be difficult except for those whom Allah guided.

questions.text: What should the believers do if the People of the Book bring every sign to them?
questions.answer: Remain firm; the People of the Book will not follow the believers' Qiblah, and the believers should not follow theirs.

questions.text: What should we say when a calamity strikes us?
questions.answer: 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' (Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return).

questions.text: What is the penalty for those who hide the 'Clear Proofs' regarding the Prophet and the Truth?
questions.answer: They are cursed by Allah and the cursers, unless they repent and make things clear.

questions.text: What is the 'sign' in the creation of the heavens and earth and the ships?
questions.answer: They are signs for a people who use their intellect ('Aql).

questions.text: How is the 'love' of the believers for Allah described compared to the polytheists' love for idols?
questions.answer: The believers are 'stronger in love for Allah'.

questions.text: What will the leaders of disbelief do to their followers on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: They will disassociate themselves from their followers and their ties will be cut.

questions.text: What are 'Halal' and 'Tayyib' things?
questions.answer: Lawful (Halal) and good/pure (Tayyib) provisions.

questions.text: What is the parable of the disbeliever who follows his ancestors blindly?
questions.answer: Like a herd that is shouted at and hears nothing but calls and cries; they are deaf, dumb, and blind.

questions.text: Is there a sin for someone who eats forbidden food under 'absolute necessity'?
questions.answer: No, provided they do not desire it or transgress the limit.

questions.text: What is the law of 'Qisas'?
questions.answer: The law of legal retribution/equality in cases of murder, meant to save lives through deterrence.

questions.text: What is the ruling on writing a 'Will' (Wasiyyah)?
questions.answer: It was prescribed for those leaving wealth to parents and near relatives, though later inheritance laws specified the shares.

questions.text: What is the 'fidyah' for those who cannot fast due to old age or chronic illness?
questions.answer: Feeding a needy person for each day missed.

questions.text: What are the 'boundaries' (Hudud) of Allah?
questions.answer: The limits set by Allah in His laws which must not be crossed.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about using wealth to bribe judges?
questions.answer: It is forbidden to consume wealth unjustly or send it to authorities to consume a portion of people's wealth sinfully.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'New Moons' (Ahillah)?
questions.answer: They are measurements of time for the people and for the Hajj.

questions.text: How should one enter houses according to the Surah?
questions.answer: Through their doors, not from the back, as was a superstitious practice during Hajj.

questions.text: What is the instruction for fighting those who fight you?
questions.answer: Fight in the way of Allah those who fight you but do not transgress; indeed, Allah does not like transgressors.

questions.text: Is 'Fitnah' (persecution/oppression) worse than killing?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Fitnah is greater than killing'.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the 'Sacred House' during Hajj?
questions.answer: Do not fight them there unless they fight you; if they cease, then Allah is Forgiving.

questions.text: What is the ruling on performing Hajj and Umrah?
questions.answer: They must be completed for Allah.

questions.text: What if a person is prevented from finishing Hajj?
questions.answer: They should offer such sacrifice as can be obtained.

questions.text: What are the forbidden acts during the months of Hajj?
questions.answer: No sexual relations (Rafath), no disobedience (Fusuq), and no disputing (Jidal).

questions.text: How should believers remember Allah at the 'Sacred Monument' (Mash'ar al-Haram)?
questions.answer: Remember Him as He has guided you, for indeed you were before that among those astray.

questions.text: What is the 'Jihaad' of the person who 'sells himself'?
questions.answer: It refers to the believer who sacrifices his life to seek the pleasure of Allah.

questions.text: What is the parable of the 'rain and the rock' regarding charity?
questions.answer: Charity done for show is like a smooth rock with dust on it; heavy rain falls and leaves it bare, yielding nothing.

questions.text: What is the parable of the 'garden on a height' regarding sincere charity?
questions.answer: It is like a fertile garden that yields double its harvest if heavy rain falls, or even a drizzle is sufficient.

questions.text: What kind of things should a believer give in charity?
questions.answer: From the good things earned and from what Allah has produced from the earth; do not aim for the bad things to give away.

questions.text: What is the 'wisdom' (Hikmah) Allah mentions in a specific ayah?
questions.answer: Allah gives it to whom He wills, and whoever is given wisdom has been given much good.

questions.text: What happens if a believer gives charity openly versus secretly?
questions.answer: Openly is good, but giving it secretly to the poor is better and expiates some sins.

questions.text: How are the 'poor who are restricted in the way of Allah' described?
questions.answer: They cannot travel for trade; the ignorant think they are wealthy because of their dignity (self-restraint), but they are known by their mark and do not beg.

questions.text: Will those who spend by night and day have fear on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: No, 'they will have their reward with their Lord'.

questions.text: Does Allah permit 'trade' but forbid 'usury' (Riba)?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Allah has permitted trade and has forbidden interest'.

questions.text: What happens to the wealth of usury versus the wealth of charity?
questions.answer: Allah destroys usury and gives increase for charities.

questions.text: What is the advice for a creditor if the debtor is in 'hardship'?
questions.answer: Let there be postponement until a time of ease, and giving it as charity is better.

questions.text: Why must debt contracts be written down?
questions.answer: To ensure justice, prevent doubts, and provide evidence.

questions.text: Is it required to write down 'immediate' trade transactions?
questions.answer: No, it is no sin if you do not write them, but have witnesses when you conclude the agreement.

questions.text: Should a scribe or witness be harmed?
questions.answer: No, they should not be harmed for doing their duty.

questions.text: What if someone is on a journey and no scribe is available for a debt?
questions.answer: A pledge/security (Rihan) should be taken.

questions.text: What is the ruling on concealing testimony?
questions.answer: It is forbidden; whoever conceals it has a sinful heart.

questions.text: Does Allah know what is in our souls even if we hide it?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Whether you show what is within yourselves or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it'.

questions.text: What are the four things the Messenger and the believers believe in?
questions.answer: Allah, His angels, His books, and His messengers.

questions.text: What is the final plea for victory at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: 'You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people'.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Baqarah teach about guidance being a mercy, not merely information?
questions.answer: It presents guidance as something that reforms the heart and life; benefiting from it requires humility, sincerity, and willingness to obey Allah.

questions.text: Why does the Surah repeatedly connect belief with action, not only statements?
questions.answer: Because true iman shows itself through worship, character, and obedience; claims without deeds are easily empty or hypocritical.

questions.text: What is one sign of hypocrisy highlighted early in the Surah that can appear in modern life?
questions.answer: Saying what sounds religious while consistently choosing dunya, lying, or harming others when it costs something to be truthful.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that sin can damage a person’s ability to recognize truth?
questions.answer: It describes hearts becoming sealed or sick when people persistently reject guidance, making repentance harder unless they return to Allah.

questions.text: What lesson does the story of Adam teach about handling mistakes?
questions.answer: Adam repented and sought Allah’s forgiveness; the right response to error is humility, remorse, and returning to Allah—not pride.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Shaytan’s strategy when he leads people into wrongdoing?
questions.answer: He beautifies sin, encourages delay in repentance, and uses doubts and desires to pull people away from obedience.

questions.text: What is the difference between ‘being tested’ and ‘being abandoned’ according to the Surah’s overall message?
questions.answer: Tests are part of faith-building; Allah still guides, teaches patience, and rewards perseverance—abandonment is when a person rejects guidance knowingly.

questions.text: How does the Surah correct the idea that ‘being chosen’ guarantees salvation regardless of deeds?
questions.answer: It stresses personal accountability: honor comes with obedience, not with lineage, slogans, or group identity.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about making religion difficult for yourself unnecessarily?
questions.answer: Obedience is required, but excessive strictness and excuses are condemned; Allah wants sincerity and compliance without stubbornness.

questions.text: What is one key character trait that the Surah praises in believers when they hear Allah’s commands?
questions.answer: Readiness to obey with humility—accepting correction and acting, rather than arguing or delaying.

questions.text: How does the Surah train a believer to balance private worship and public responsibility?
questions.answer: It combines inner faith, prayer, and sincerity with social duties like justice, charity, and protecting rights.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the spiritual danger of showing off in charity?
questions.answer: It can erase reward; charity should be for Allah, with kindness and dignity, not for reputation or control.

questions.text: Why does the Surah emphasize giving from what is good and lawful rather than what is leftover or disliked?
questions.answer: Because Allah is pure and deserves sincerity; giving low-quality charity reflects stinginess and weak gratitude.

questions.text: What is a practical meaning of ‘spending in Allah’s way’ beyond money?
questions.answer: It includes time, skills, effort, and support for family and community in halal ways for Allah’s pleasure.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the mindset of someone who sincerely seeks Allah’s approval?
questions.answer: They aim for Allah’s pleasure even when unseen, accept hardship, and keep their intentions clean from ego and manipulation.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about repentance after major sins?
questions.answer: The door remains open if repentance is sincere—regret, stopping the sin, returning to obedience, and making amends where needed.

questions.text: How does the Surah protect people from spiritual despair?
questions.answer: By showing Allah’s mercy, opening repentance, and teaching that guidance and forgiveness are available to those who turn back sincerely.

questions.text: What is one misconception about dua that Surah Al-Baqarah corrects?
questions.answer: That dua is only for big crises; it teaches calling on Allah consistently with trust, patience, and lawful effort.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that Islamic law aims to protect people, not punish them?
questions.answer: Many rulings are tied to mercy, preventing harm, protecting families, and building a just community.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about alcohol and gambling as social harms, not only personal sins?
questions.answer: They create addiction, conflict, financial ruin, and broken families; the Surah frames them as major sources of فساد (corruption).

questions.text: How does the Surah guide Muslims on handling money ethically?
questions.answer: It encourages honest trade, fulfilling contracts, avoiding exploitation, and refusing interest-based ظلم that traps the vulnerable.

questions.text: What is the spiritual difference between charity and interest-based profit according to the Surah’s message?
questions.answer: Charity purifies wealth and heart; interest feeds greed and injustice, harming society and hardening the soul.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about documenting financial obligations?
questions.answer: To prevent disputes and ظلم: write clearly, be just, use witnesses when possible, and avoid ambiguity.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Baqarah teach the importance of trustworthy witnesses and honesty in testimony?
questions.answer: It treats truthful testimony as a religious duty; hiding or twisting it is a sin that harms justice.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about reconciliation in family conflicts?
questions.answer: Seek fairness, avoid ظلم, and aim for peaceful solutions; separation may be allowed but should not be done with cruelty or revenge.

questions.text: How does the Surah protect women’s dignity in marriage and divorce matters?
questions.answer: It sets boundaries against harm, commands kind treatment, and requires responsibility and fairness rather than using divorce as a weapon.

questions.text: What is one principle the Surah teaches about consent and kindness in marriage?
questions.answer: Marriage should be built on lawful commitment, respect, and mutual rights—harm and coercion contradict Islamic character.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach believers to deal with uncertainty about the future?
questions.answer: Rely on Allah, take lawful means, and remember that provision and outcomes are in Allah’s hand; anxiety should not lead to haram.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the value of patience when facing community pressure?
questions.answer: Truth may be unpopular; believers are asked to remain steady, prioritize Allah’s approval, and avoid being dragged by people’s expectations.

questions.text: How does the Surah define true righteousness (birr) as more than rituals?
questions.answer: It includes faith, prayer, charity, fulfilling promises, patience, and moral integrity—not only outward directions or symbols.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about fulfilling promises, whether to Allah or people?
questions.answer: Promises are a trust; breaking them damages faith and society. Believers are commanded to honor commitments and be reliable.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach a believer to respond when they see ظلم in society?
questions.answer: Stand for justice with wisdom, avoid participating in oppression, and support what is right through lawful means and sincere intention.

questions.text: What is one key lesson from the Surah’s repeated mention of Allah’s knowledge?
questions.answer: Allah knows hidden motives and private choices; sincerity matters and self-deception will not benefit on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that knowledge without humility can become harmful?
questions.answer: People may argue, twist words, or use knowledge for ego; true knowledge leads to submission, honesty, and fear of Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about respecting places of worship and avoiding corruption in them?
questions.answer: Preventing worship or harming sacred spaces is a grave ظلم; believers are taught to honor worship and protect community access to it.

questions.text: How does the Surah guide believers about food and consumption habits?
questions.answer: Eat what is lawful and wholesome, avoid excess, and don’t let desire lead to haram or ingratitude.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about leadership and responsibility, based on its stories and laws?
questions.answer: Authority is a trust from Allah; leaders must be just, accountable, and protective of people’s rights—not arrogant or self-serving.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach a believer to think about death and the Hereafter in daily decisions?
questions.answer: It makes accountability real: choices about money, family, and worship should be made with the Hereafter in mind, not only short-term gain.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Baqarah teach about seeking Allah’s help in hardship?
questions.answer: Seek help through patience and prayer, maintain hope, and remember that Allah supports those who remain steadfast.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about responding to Allah’s favors with gratitude rather than entitlement?
questions.answer: Gratitude means recognizing blessings from Allah, using them in obedience, and avoiding arrogance and complaint when tested.
